qa_pair.question: For matter <display_number>, how many tasks with status "Pending" are due on or before <date>?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters (read its display_number to resolve the numeric matter id) → clio_list_tasks(matter_id=<id>, status="Pending", due_date_end=<date>, limit=200) → count the returned tasks.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after pointing at a known matter and date.

qa_pair.question: For matter <display_number>, what is the total outstanding balance across its issued bills (in the firm's currency)?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters → resolve <display_number> to the matter id → clio_get_billing_summary(matter_id=<id>) → read total_outstanding (draft/void bills are already excluded by the tool).
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after pointing at a known matter with bills.

qa_pair.question: For matter <display_number>, what is the total number of hours logged as time entries dated between <start_date> and <end_date> inclusive?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters → resolve <display_number> to the matter id → clio_list_time_entries(matter_id=<id>, start_date=<start_date>, end_date=<end_date>, limit=200) → sum quantity_in_hours across the returned entries.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills (pick a window with ≤200 entries so the single page is complete).

qa_pair.question: On matter <display_number>, how many Pending tasks are assigned to the attorney named "<full_name>"?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters → resolve <display_number> to the matter id; clio_list_users(name="<full_name>") → resolve the attorney's user id; clio_list_tasks(matter_id=<id>, status="Pending", limit=200) → count tasks whose assignee.id equals that user id.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after choosing a matter and an attorney on it.

qa_pair.question: For the client whose contact record lists the email <email>, how many of the firm's currently open matters belong to that client?
qa_pair.method: clio_search_contacts(query="<email>") → read the matching contact's canonical name; clio_list_matters(status="open", limit=200) → count matters whose client equals that name. (list_matters has no page cursor — verify the firm has ≤200 open matters so the single page is complete.)
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after choosing a client with at least one open matter.

qa_pair.question: What is the name of the most recently created document on matter <display_number>?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters → resolve <display_number> to the matter id → clio_list_documents(matter_id=<id>, limit=200) → pick the document with the latest created_at → return its name.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after pointing at a matter that has documents.

qa_pair.question: How many calendar entries associated with matter <display_number> fall between <from> and <to> inclusive?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters → resolve <display_number> to the matter id; clio_list_calendar_entries(from=<from>, to=<to>) → count entries whose matter.display_number equals <display_number>.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after choosing a matter and a date range with hearings/deadlines.

qa_pair.question: On matter <display_number>, how many distinct firm users logged time entries between <start_date> and <end_date> inclusive?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_matters → resolve <display_number> to the matter id → clio_list_time_entries(matter_id=<id>, start_date=<start_date>, end_date=<end_date>, limit=200) → count distinct user.id across the returned entries.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills (pick a window with ≤200 entries so the single page is complete).

qa_pair.question: What is the primary phone number on file for the contact named "<contact_name>"?
qa_pair.method: clio_search_contacts(query="<contact_name>") → resolve the contact id → clio_get_contact(contact_id=<id>) → return the first phone_numbers entry's number.
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after choosing a contact with a phone number on file.

qa_pair.question: How many total hours did the user named "<full_name>" log across all matters between <start_date> and <end_date> inclusive?
qa_pair.method: clio_list_users(name="<full_name>") → resolve the user id; clio_list_time_entries(start_date=<start_date>, end_date=<end_date>, limit=200) → sum quantity_in_hours over entries whose user.id equals that user id. (list_time_entries has no page cursor — pick a window with ≤200 firm-wide entries so the page is complete.)
qa_pair.answer: FIRM-SPECIFIC — operator fills after choosing a timekeeper and a bounded window.
